stories.headline: Oil prices rise despite Iran’s proposal to reopen Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Brent crude rises 3 percent despite Iran's offer to reopen waterway in exchange for deferral of nuclear talks.

stories.headline: Iran to reopen Strait of Hormuz if US lifts blockade, reports claim
stories.summary: Despite an ongoing ceasefire, the US and Iran are locked in a standoff over the Strait of Hormuz, through which a fifth of the world’s oil and gas trade passes in peacetime.

stories.headline: Palestine weekly wrap: Under cover of ceasefire, Israel increases grip
stories.summary: Settler violence surges deeper into Palestinian-administered areas, and Gaza's police forces are decimated further.

stories.summary: Trump team reviews Iran peace plan to reopen Hormuz with nuclear talks potentially delayed to a later stage.

stories.headline: Trumps greet King Charles III and Queen Camilla at the White House
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump and First Lady Melania Trump greet Britain's King Charles III and Queen Camilla upon arrival at the South Portico of the White House in Washington, DC.

stories.headline: Russian superyacht sails through Strait of Hormuz despite blockade
stories.summary: The 141m-long vessel, linked to a close ally of Russian President Vladimir Putin, cleared the waterway despite an ongoing blockade.

stories.headline: Shin-Etsu Withholds Outlook Due to Iran War Price Disruption
stories.summary: Shin-Etsu Chemical Co. withheld its full-year forecast, citing supply constraints and price fluctuations stemming from the war in the Middle East.

stories.headline: At least 20 killed in Colombia highway blast
stories.summary: Colombia's President Gustavo Petro has blamed a 'narco-terrorist group', led by a former FARC fighter, for the attack.

stories.headline: Suspect in press gala shooting charged with attempting to kill Trump
stories.summary: Cole Tomas Allen was officially charged with trying to assassinate US President Donald Trump.

stories.headline: EU energy mix: Crisis pushes balance on nuclear, renewables and security
stories.summary: The EU is pivoting back to nuclear after decades of hesitation, driven by the dual shock of the energy crisis and the realisation that renewables alone can't power a heavy industrial economy.

stories.headline: What we're learning about suspected Washington press dinner gunman
stories.summary: The man arrested at the event attended by President Trump appeared in court on Monday.

stories.headline: NZ axes plan for WW2 sex slaves statue after Japan protest
stories.summary: Tokyo had warned that installing the structure in Auckland could jeopardise diplomatic relations.

stories.headline: Barclays takes £228mn hit from collapse of UK mortgage lender MFS
stories.summary: Bank to limit complex lending activities and reduce exposure to highly leveraged corporates

stories.headline: Swiss Fund Topping 95% of Peers Sees Room for Oil Stocks to Run
stories.summary: A Swiss value investor who has outperformed most peers this year after sticking with his bet on energy stocks says the rally has further to run, as share prices catch up with earnings upgrades spurred by the Iran war.

stories.headline: BP Oil Trading Revenue Boosted by Iran War
stories.summary: BP said earnings jumped in the first quarter as spiraling energy prices and market turmoil triggered by the Iran war led to a surge in profits from its oil trading operation. Bloomberg's Mitchell Ferman reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: King Charles Will Speak of ‘Reconciliation and Renewal’ During Address to Congress
stories.summary: The state visit of King Charles III comes at a moment of tension over the war in Iran between President Trump and Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

stories.headline: New Zealand spy plane reports possible North Korea sanctions breach at sea
stories.summary: New Zealand's Defence Force says a North Korean ship was observed engaging in a possible transfer of illicit goods at sea.

stories.headline: US political violence generates a familiar cycle - this time it's in overdrive
stories.summary: In modern America, it seems violence of this kind has become an ever-present storm that can strike anywhere and at any moment.

stories.headline: Foreign Office not consulted ahead of Mandelson appointment
stories.summary: Vote on whether prime minister misled parliament will follow testimony

stories.headline: Ping An Profit Falls as Market Decline Hurts Investment Returns
stories.summary: Ping An Insurance (Group) Co. said profit fell 7.4% in the first quarter, as declines in China’s stock market dented investment returns.

stories.headline: EQT Raises €3.1 Billion for New European Real Estate Fund
stories.summary: EQT AB has raised €3.1 billion ($3.6 billion) for its latest European real estate fund despite a challenging fundraising climate for the wider sector.

stories.headline: Hedge Fund Short Covering Fuels Demand in $5 Billion CATL Deal
stories.summary: Hedge funds bought the bulk of the shares in Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd.’s $5 billion placement, with at least some of the demand driven by traders seeking to cover short positions on the battery maker’s Hong Kong-listed shares, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

stories.headline: Strategists See Yen Slipping as Ueda Softens Hawkish Tone
stories.summary: Market strategists said Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda refraining from giving a decisive signal about the timing of a rate hike indicated that the BOJ may not be as hawkish as its split vote suggested.

stories.headline: JPMorgan AM, MFS Bet on European Debt After Sharp Selloff
stories.summary: Investors including JPMorgan Asset Management, BlackRock Inc. and MFS Investment Management are buying shorter-term European government debt in an effort to lock in yields before they slip away.

stories.headline: Yen Slips as BOJ’s Ueda Fails to Give Clear Rate-Hike Signal
stories.summary: The yen turned lower against the dollar after Governor Kazuo Ueda refrained from giving a decisive signal about the timing of a rate hike following the Bank of Japan’s split decision to policy unchanged.

stories.headline: Netherlands Rejects Argentina Soy Meal Cargoes Over GMO Issue
stories.summary: The Netherlands has rejected at least two Argentinian soybean meal cargoes this month after non-approved genetically modified material was found, threatening to disrupt a major trade flow.

stories.headline: China Forces Meta to Unwind AI Deal as BOJ Turns More Hawkish | Insight with Haslinda Amin 4/28/2026
stories.summary: Insight with Haslinda Amin, a daily news program featuring in-depth, high-profile interviews and analysis to give viewers the complete picture on the stories that matter. The show features prominent leaders spanning the worlds of business, finance, politics and culture. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: ECB Shouldn't Think About Raising Rates, BNY Says
stories.summary: Geoffrey Yu, senior market strategist at BNY, discusses the outlook for the energy sector and how it could impact Thursday's interest-rate decision by the European Central Bank. "The last thing that the ECB should be thinking about, I think, is tightening rates," he tells Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Man pleads guilty in the 2002 killing of Run-DMC's Jam Master Jay
stories.summary: Jay Bryant has pleaded guilty to a federal murder charge, admitting to a judge he helped people get into a building so the Run-DMC band member Jam Master Jay could be ambushed in his recording studio in 2002.

stories.headline: The World Cup is driving a new kind of tourism in Mexico: Working remotely and living football for weeks at a time
stories.summary: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is driving the arrival of European digital nomads in Mexico, a phenomenon transforming tourism with longer stays, remote working and a greater impact on the local economy.

stories.headline: Oil Back Above $110 as World Awaits US Response to Iran Proposal
stories.summary: Oil rose to a three-week high above $110 a barrel in London as traders awaited the US response to a proposal from Tehran to end the war and reopen the crucial Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: US strike kills three on alleged narco boat as campaign death toll hits 185

stories.headline: Colombia offers record $1.4m-reward for rebel it blames for deadly bomb attack
stories.summary: Officials say a rebel leader known as "Marlon" ordered the attack in which 20 people were killed.

stories.headline: Kim Jong Un opens memorial for N Korean soldiers killed in Ukraine war
stories.summary: Kim Jong Un and Russian Defence Minister Andrey Belousov unveiled the memorial in Pyongyang.

stories.headline: Vietnam Gas Major Looks to US as Iran War Reorders LPG Flows
stories.summary: PetroVietnam Gas JSC plans to import more liquefied petroleum gas from the US than its traditional Middle East suppliers next month, in the latest sign that the Iran war is reordering global energy flows.

stories.headline: King Charles Visits Trump’s Washington After Chaos From Press Dinner Shooting
stories.summary: Presidents use state visits to show off all that is appealing about American culture. But the shooting at the White House correspondents’ dinner has cast a shadow over the visit.

stories.headline: Republicans Push for Trump’s White House Ballroom After Gala Attack
stories.summary: The attack on a press dinner in Washington, which is being called an attempted assassination of President Trump, has also renewed the fight over reopening the Homeland Security Department.

stories.headline: What I Saw in the Room With Trump at DC Gala Shooting
stories.summary:
The White House will review security at major events involving President Trump — after a gunman targeted the White House Correspondents' Dinner.

Mishal Husain explains the impact on King Charles' state visit. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Investigators in Trump Assassination Attempt Look Through Bluesky for Suspect’s Motive
stories.summary: Federal authorities are looking into whether Cole Tomas Allen posted on Bluesky as “coldforce,” who wrote and promoted liberal views that did not stand out on the left-leaning platform.

stories.headline: Key Labour MPs start to accept need for UK welfare reforms

stories.headline: Tech Drags Stocks Lower as Brent Tops $110 Again: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Technology stocks led equity markets lower as concerns over whether the vast amount of investment in artificial intelligence will pay off resurfaced. Brent rose above $110 a barrel as the Strait of Hormuz stayed shut.

stories.headline: Number of executions in North Korea rose dramatically during Covid – report

stories.headline: Netanyahu orders army to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.summary: The Israeli PM's directive comes as six people were killed by strikes in Lebanon despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

stories.headline: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
stories.summary: Iran had earlier said there were no plans for a direct meeting with a US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.

stories.headline: Palestinians in West Bank and some in Gaza vote in local elections
stories.summary: Local elections have been held in the occupied West Bank and in one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups are not taking part.

stories.headline: BBC visits Chernobyl ghost city 40 years after world's worst nuclear accident
stories.summary: The BBC's Jessica Parker visits Pripyat, which was abandoned in 1986 after an explosion at the nearby Chernobyl nuclear power plant.

stories.headline: Two killed in strike on alleged drug boat in Pacific Ocean, US military says
stories.summary: The incident is the latest in a series of US strikes on alleged drug trafficking boats.

stories.headline: Mexico says US agents killed in crash weren't permitted to operate there

stories.headline: Orbán steps down from Hungarian parliament after landslide defeat
stories.summary: The outgoing prime minister will not take up his seat after leading his party back into opposition.

stories.headline: Katya Adler: Europe's Nato allies push back at reported US threat to Spain

stories.headline: Nato says 'no provision' to expel members after report US could seek to suspend Spain
stories.summary: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlines options to punish allies over a perceived lack of support for Iran war.

stories.headline: Thousands at risk after multi-million dollar Everest flood warning system left to rust

stories.headline: Headscarf with a beret: Muslim designers showcase floral dresses and boxy streetwear in Paris

stories.headline: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu treated for early-stage prostate cancer

stories.headline: Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike

stories.headline: Iran says Strait of Hormuz cannot be opened due to ceasefire breaches
